note: Fathom meeting transcripts + distilled context Alex pastes in. Each entry: date, title, attendees, tags, summary, and notes (markdown). Used as context for ads, offers, messaging. Speaker diarization is unreliable — store team CONCLUSIONS, not who-said-which-quote.

meetings.title: Tyson $1k/day push — lead magnet relaunch + Direct CTA double (Matt + Will)
meetings.creator: Alex · Matt · Will · ~35m
meetings.summary: Push Tyson to $1,000/day for 7 days: relaunch Summer Shred lead magnet ($300/day, normal system, zero Zakk) + double the Direct CTA (~7 ads varying the FIT winner into distinct visual lanes, $50/ad, one-ad-per-ad-set). Breakeven ~4x; bottleneck is lead flow not closing; Keith offboarding frees Gideon + Kels.
meetings.notes:
## The decision — Tyson to $1,000/day for 7 days
1. **Lead magnet (Summer Shred) $300/day — Alex's NORMAL system, ZERO Zakk:** ONE ad set, ~25 ads, warm audience (refreshed), STORY PLACEMENT ONLY, IG-text-on-image style ONLY, NO AI creative (AI only to mimic the text style). Grab winners from recent + prior lead-magnet campaigns, fill with variations. Matt: relaunch has worked every time (5-7x, only need 4x) — don't change the process; avoid effort-bias shortcuts AND over-engineering.
2. **Direct CTA double via ~7 ads @ $50/day, one ad per ad set (Zakk).** Same winning copy/offer = the FIT '5 servicemen' winner, diversified into distinct VISUAL lanes (carousel, B-roll, AI high-CTR graphic, different placements). Zakk methods welcome HERE — it's max spend on one proven winner.

## The math / why $1k
- Breakeven ~**4x** = ~$4k/day avg for the month. June running ~$4,324/day. May ~4-5x.
- Real driver: feed freed-up setters — Keith offboarding frees **Gideon** (+ Kels) for lead-magnet leads (~50/day each) and **doubles Amara's** volume. Reverse-math ~= $1k/day.
- **Bottleneck has ALWAYS been LEAD FLOW, not closing** (Will's analytics: every slowdown = leads, not close rate). Fill the funnel.

## Will's read (sales side)
- Confident now: 3 strong closers (Will/Broz/Austin) + 2 new (one ex-Navy, Will-trained), who'll do outbound dialing by standard.
- Risk: higher spend magnifies team mistakes (the 2-day cash drop spooked Alex). Both outcomes win: 4x+ -> scale; miss -> learn what breaks (~$7k 'tuition').
- Follow-up dialing system for scheduled/talked leads on slow days/weekends.

## Cadence + strategy
- **Decide at 5-7 days, NOT daily.** Analyze each ad's performance AND why expected winners under-delivered (budget starvation / attention).
- **'Catch the drift' (Will):** proactively RELAUNCH old winners ~2 weeks in BEFORE current ads fatigue; keep both running so one ramps as the other trickles = smoother lead flow (vs the every-3-week relaunch-after-crash cycle).

## ManyChat / attribution
- New **Summer Shred** lead-magnet automation to be (re)made (archived; ~5 versions). **Route 50% to Gideon** (Matt).
- **CMO flag (mine):** every ManyChat flow that fires a webhook must pass {{custom_field.keyword}} (esp. lead_engaged) or keywords leak at $1k/day exactly when clean data matters for the 7-day call. Lucy's flows = 0% leak; replicate.

## Shipped from this call
- Lead magnet LIVE 2026-06-06: 14 ads, $300/day, greetings correct. Direct CTA double still pending.
meetings.archive: captured live in this CMO chat 2026-06-06 (Fathom: fathom.video/share/yYPZoN-rtBLg2KLH2ZivZoSt661xaR_L)

meetings.title: Team strategy — Lucy/Keith/Tyson ads, DM accountability, incentives
meetings.creator: Alex · Matt · Will · (3h)
meetings.summary: Re-targeted every creator's ICP, designed a DM follow-up accountability system, validated the Sales Hub numbers, and landed on a focused weekend plan: push Lucy only, hold Tyson + Keith until Sunday.
meetings.notes:
## The plan they landed on (this week)
*(Final order of operations — this SUPERSEDED an earlier idea in the call to double Tyson + route 20% to Kels; the weekend-calendar reasoning below changed the call.)*

1. **Lucy is priority #1.** Launch new Lucy direct-CTA ads — 5 angles (below) in ONE ad set, let the algorithm spend, then force spend to the winner. Ramp to **$300/day** (not $500 yet — too new). Run it through the weekend (2–3 days), watch **day-over-day** + decide on **7 days**.
2. **Will's only job this week:** be in Lucy's DMs, make sure **Debbie** is dialed, sales team on point. If sales is solid, Alex is freed to execute ads.
3. **Do NOT touch Tyson or Keith spend until Sunday.** Reason: weekend calendar. Upping spend Fri books calls into a weekend with no closer coverage. Sunday → raise Tyson + Keith.
4. **Install the next closer for next weekend** (solves the weekend-availability hole). Will scheduling interviews.
5. **Keith:** change nothing yet — Matt's Keith data isn't dialed; Alex + Matt review Keith numbers together, report to Will.

## Current read per creator (ICP + ad angles)
- **Tyson** — the proven engine. Alpha-male voice, direct-CTA working (~4x). Note: team feels his real persona is softer than the alpha positioning, which may cap it.
- **Keith** — current 'high-value' targeting is WRONG. Real audience: younger, **mostly females love him**; the males are beginners/lost-their-gym-support-group, seeking community. New direction: emotional + beginner + community ('feel lost in the gym', 'lost your support system', 'going but not seeing results'). Data confirms: lead-magnet works (~6–7x), direct-CTA failing (~0.7–1.5x) → fix the messaging, keep the lead magnet.
- **Lucy** — NOT 'female Tyson'. ICP: Whole Foods + faith + lifting + running, looks great, 25, herniated-disc injury → lost her physique → faith/gratitude origin. **5 ad angles to test (direct CTA):** (1) Food — whole foods vs 'health foods', 'eating right but not seeing results'; (2) Running — runners are lean-but-soft, 'running to lose fat but looking worse'; (3) Faith — temple/God's design + whole foods (ONLY with Lucy's approval, handle with care); (4) Weight training — women's fear of getting bulky / don't know how to start; (5) Her story — injury → lost it all → found herself (God + self-accountability) → coaching. Female pain points: bloating, energy, PCOS, endometriosis, hormones, the 3-good-weeks/1-bad-week cycle. Copy: short (3rd–5th grade reading, 2–3s attention), CTA leaves suspense.
- **Antoine** — onboards in ~10 days. ICP: older alpha males like Tyson, 150–200k+ earners / CEO-level / family, 'lost everything physically, have everything materially'. Soften his hard IFBB-pro look; 'anomaly' angle ('I'm top 1% genetics — you can be top 10%'). Alex finds him easiest (Tyson-like voice).

## Operational builds decided
- **'Go Hunt / Time to Eat' DM accountability system (Matt to build):** if a lead replies and isn't answered within ~24h → tag it → a centralized **DMs tab** (new standalone tab in the app) shows 'new opportunity' deep-links; opening one reassigns the lead to whoever grabs it (ManyChat reassigns on reply). 2nd offense → 'dead meat' for accountability. Needs: all setters on all accounts (+~$300–500/mo ManyChat seats), stored ManyChat user IDs, and **response-time tracking (11am–11pm window)** per company/client/setter.
- **Setter inbox fix:** Gideon (Keith) has 383 open convos = disorganized, dropping even 2-day-old leads. Rule: close anything >5 days. Follow-up VA trained Sun, live Mon.
- **Kels = floater** across all 3 accounts (~50 new leads/day, ~20% of direct-CTA), since Amara is bandwidth-capped on Tyson CTA and is the strongest setter; Kels is the test case for the new setup.

## Strategy / doctrine in action
- **More → Better → New, live:** a 'push straight to checkout / free-community' idea was correctly judged 'a lot of new' and shelved — do the simplest thing first (just change Lucy/Keith targeting inside the existing DM funnel), revisit new in 2–3 weeks if still believed.
- **The constraint right now = setter time/bandwidth** (not budget). Every ads decision (direct-CTA vs lead-magnet, how much to spend) gates on it.
- **Ad-testing method:** many angles × 3–5 creatives, ~$100–150/day test, REMOVE losers (don't just keep winners), force spend to winners, decide every 5–7 days.
- **Business model:** influencers pay nothing upfront; CCOS fronts ad spend, invoices expenses, splits profit 50/50 (keeps more for the risk). Extremely sticky → near-zero churn. Debated a 15–100k setup fee, decided getting them in beats the upfront cash.
- **Incentives (Will to propose):** weekly + monthly closer competitions, team + individual, promote-from-within ladder (assistant sales mgr). Records: Will's best day $9,997 (Feb 4); 2026 avg week ≈ $17.7k cash. Will wants a partner path.

## My independent read / flags
- The Keith conclusion (lead-magnet works, fix messaging, hold) lines up with my earlier kill-the-cold-direct-CTA analysis — consistent.
- **The 5 Lucy angles are HYPOTHESES, not proven** — they come from Will's sales experience + 'the boys agree' + a few closed females, which Alex explicitly pushed on for statistical significance. The plan correctly treats them as a test; judge on real cost-per-lead / cost-per-call over 7 days.
- The Sales Hub show-rate was validated in the call (the 58% vs 72% gap was a word-miscalc bug; manual math = 53.57% matched the dashboard). Good — the numbers source is trustworthy, with that one fix noted.

meetings.title: Antwan onboarding interview — story, ICP, voice (for ad messaging)
meetings.creator: Alex · Matt with Antwan · 78m
meetings.summary: Antwan Barrow (brand 'Antwan Rarcus' — his dad's middle name) — new creator. Deep authentic-story interview to mine ad messaging. Ideal client = men ~23-40 (entrepreneurs/business pros) who have money+family but lack the body/discipline/confidence to lead; want to become a better MAN, not just a transformation. DROP 'alpha male' (he dislikes it) → 'best self / better man'. Action item: draft ads -> send Antwan for approval.
meetings.notes:
## ICP + messaging (the gold for ads)
- **Ideal client: men ~23-40 who want to become a better version of themselves ENTIRELY — a better man/human, not just a body transformation.** Entrepreneurs / business pros who HAVE the money + family but LACK the body card / face card, the structure, discipline, and confidence to lead and command respect. (Matt mid-call: 'sounds like you just wrote the first ad.')
- **DROP 'alpha male'** — Antwan explicitly dislikes the term. Frame as 'best self / better man.'
- **Core mindset gap = belief.** Most just don't believe they CAN ('I can't, so why try'; excuses: too busy, bad shoulders). First + biggest hurdle is believing it's possible.
- His non-negotiable: gym 5x/week = discipline/structure that earns his wife's trust ('if I'm disciplined here, she trusts I'm disciplined in our relationship'). Clapback on a '5x is egotistical' hater: 'it just means they want it more than you.'

## Anti-positioning (what he stands AGAINST — use for authority/contrast)
- Coaches who emotionally rob people with one-trick / 'unique diet or training' result promises.
- People who get enhancements/surgery then sell 'look like me if you do this.'
- Unqualified coaches who lost the weight via ANOTHER coach, then market it as self-made.
- He's openly an enhanced athlete and would NEVER claim 'do what I did -> look like me' (you'd need his genetics, mindset, trauma, resilience). 'Honesty is the best medicine; a lie is just a band-aid — the wound's still open.'

## Story beats (raw material for ad angles + his real voice)
- Born 1990, Colorado Springs. Very young parents (dad 15, mom 16), split household. Dad a gangbanger; grew up around gang violence / shootouts / robberies; raised by extended family + grandma ('second mom'). Rowdy/aggressive kid.
- Sports were his outlet (football every position, track, hoops); star at Fountain HS until 9th grade.
- **Turning point:** stepmom robbed the family (gave the mortgage money to her ex-con fiancé) -> 2009 crash -> dad lost the 3-story home -> **his dad attempted suicide in front of him** (pills at the table + a 'proud of you' speech; Antwan grabbed him, they cried, dad chose to live for his son). Lesson he carries: life is fragile, 'be on your shit and enjoy it because it can be stripped away.' (Also a guarded 'great men fall from women' theme.)
- Joined the **Air Force** (military family lineage) -> discipline, structure, punctuality ('10 min early or you're late'), camaraderie. Spec Ops C-130 care-package squadron, England 3 yrs, 18 countries. Stayed fit throughout (PT leader).
- San Diego on the GI Bill (National University, grad 2019). 'Self-Made Training Facility' -> lead trainer + gym manager, learned business ops. Got into bodybuilding (coached free) — took ~3 yrs to learn the game (1st in class, 2nd overall repeatedly) before winning + turning pro.
- **2019 = the crucible:** graduated college + lacerated his thumb tendon (kept training through the cast — wove bands/kettlebells into it, aired the sweaty cast after cardio) + **his mom died (49, stroke, 90% brain gray; he + his brother chose to pull the cord).** He'd signed her up for a free gym/trainer 'a day too late' -> deep guilt -> THE driver behind 'I advocate for health.' His coach talked him out of quitting ('if you stop now everything about you changes'). Lessons: 'your body gives up before your mind does'; 'sometimes the win is the character, not the trophy.'
- Conviction (strong/controversial): neglecting your health = selfish + toxic, affects your kids + spouse. 'How could you not love yourself enough to want to live?'
- Turned PRO, married. Wife (big IG account, followed <100 people) followed HIM after his Olympia post; he DM'd ('do I know you?' -> 'you're a snack plus two') -> FaceTimed -> first convo was a 3-hr call -> she flew to see him + meet his family within a week -> tattooed his initial on her finger within months. 'I don't believe in chances, I believe in faith.' Tesla (dream Challenger was stolen on his birthday), bought/sold a house, moved to Portland (wife at OHSU, 4.0, going for valedictorian).

## Voice / persona for copy
Tough, raw, authentic, faith-driven, military discipline, resilient-through-trauma, honor + community-protective, 'be on your shit.' A quote machine. NOT 'alpha male.'

## Ties to
Tomorrow's Antwan launch (the $500/day aggressive $60k/mo push) — this is the source material for his ad copy; draft -> send Antwan for approval.

meetings.title: Zakk Colburn coaching call (ads scaling)
meetings.creator: Alex + Matt with Zakk (coach) · 46m
meetings.summary: Zakk's exact method for scaling DM ads: one campaign, one ad set per ad at $50/day, scale until KPIs break, judge weekly, relaunch old winners, and how to vary a winning creative.
meetings.notes:
## Who
Zakk Colburn is Alex's paid ADS coach (external, not part of the business). This call is the source of the kill/scale + scaling rules.

## KPIs (priority order)
1. **Profit is the #1 KPI** — optimize cash COLLECTED (not revenue), 2x+ on a 30-day window (2x floor, often much higher for fitness DM ads).
2. **CAC <= 20% of ticket.** Alex ticket $2,400 -> CAC target <= $480.
3. **Cost per booked call = CAC x close rate.** Alex closes 56% on ads -> can book a call up to ~$270. (Alex's actual ~$40/call at 7-10x ROAS — way under target.)

## Structure + scaling
- ONE campaign total per account. Every ad in its OWN ad set with its OWN $50/day budget. Let them all play out, scale the in-KPI ones, slowly kill the rest.
- Launch new ads at $50/day (Meta's floor). Scale 50 -> 100 -> 200 -> 300 -> 400... in ~weekly steps until KPIs break, then step back to the last good budget.
- **Budget = a commitment, not a cap.** Any ad >= 2x cash ROI, rip it past budget.
- **Decide every Sunday** on 7-14d performance. Kill out-of-KPI ads, move freed spend into winners + a slice into testing.
- **Relaunch old winners:** keep a monthly written list of best ads; relaunching a past winner has a very high hit-rate when low on creative. (Saved Zakk a dead month.)
- **Lead-quality kill:** if KPIs look fine but nothing closes, the leads are trash — kill and relaunch proven winners.

## How to vary a winning image ad
Same copy + different image; same copy as a carousel (each line a slide); same copy over B-roll video; change the person (gender/age); a talking-head version; and the big one: **write a tailored ad per ICP from your best clients' sales-call language** (feed real sales calls to Claude, one ad per persona), keeping the proven winning FORMAT. Creative is usually the ceiling, not audience size (Tyson's retargeting pool alone ~2.6M).

## My note
These rules are baked into the Kill / Scale Decision skill + the Ad Copywriter skill. Full verbatim call is in the private deep archive.
